machinery_genes.role: spike-sharpening tip protein encoded within the Aux3 cluster (sharpens the VgrG tail-spike; loads the Aux3 effector)
machinery_genes.quote: VCA0284, which encodes the larger of the two PAAR proteins identified so far in V. cholerae
machinery_genes.note: PAAR-2 = VCA0284, the only apparatus gene physically encoded in the Aux3 module itself (Aux3 = VCA0281 integrase, VCA0282 IS5 transposase, VCA0284 PAAR2, VCA0285 TseH effector, VCA0286 TsiH immunity). Santoriello 2020 (PMID 33288753) confirms placement: 'the genes encoding PAAR2, TseH and TsiH (VCA0284-VCA0286)'. The curated effector for this instance, VC_A0285 = TseH, is delivered by this PAAR tip. Effector TseH and immunity TsiH deliberately excluded as non-machinery.

machinery_genes.role: hexameric inner tube (secreted); the conduit the Aux3 effector travels through
machinery_genes.quote: potential effector proteins secreted by this pathway (i.e., hemolysin-coregulated protein and VgrG)
machinery_genes.note: Pukatzki et al 2006 PNAS, the founding V. cholerae T6SS (VAS) paper. Hcp = hemolysin-coregulated protein. Encoded in the large/main T6SS cluster, not in Aux3, but is the shared secreted tube on which all V. cholerae clusters (including Aux3) depend. Listed per the T6SS-SPECIAL rule (Hcp/VgrG/PAAR are machinery).

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike / cell-puncturing device (tube tip), secreted
machinery_genes.quote: In Vibrio cholerae, T6SS have been found to secrete three related proteins extracellularly, VgrG-1, VgrG-2, and VgrG-3.
machinery_genes.note: Pukatzki et al 2007 PNAS. VgrG-1 (VC1416) is the actin-cross-linking spike of the large cluster; named here as a secreted VgrG spike component that, with VgrG-2/3, assembles the cell-puncturing device. Shared apparatus delivering all V. cholerae T6SS effectors including the Aux3 TseH.

machinery_genes.quote: VgrG-1 was shown to interact with itself, VgrG-2, and VgrG-3, suggesting that such a complex does form.
machinery_genes.note: Pukatzki et al 2007 PNAS. VgrG-2 = VCA0018, a large-cluster spike protein; one of the three V. cholerae VgrG components forming the trimeric tail-spike.

machinery_genes.quote: In Vibrio cholerae, T6SS have been found to secrete three related proteins extracellularly, VgrG-1, VgrG-2, and VgrG-3.
machinery_genes.note: Pukatzki et al 2007 PNAS. VgrG-3 = VCA0123. Named here as a secreted VgrG spike component of the V. cholerae T6SS; the VgrG-3 C-terminal domain carries a separate cell-wall-degrading effector activity, but the VgrG-3 spike itself is apparatus.

machinery_genes.role: contractile sheath subunit (inner); forms tubular VipA/VipB sheath around the Hcp tube
machinery_genes.quote: ClpV ... interacts through its N-terminal domain with the VipA/VipB complex that is composed of two conserved and essential members of T6SS.
machinery_genes.note: Bonemann et al 2009 EMBO J, V. cholerae. VipA = TssB sheath subunit. Large-cluster structural component; shared sheath that powers firing of all V. cholerae T6SS effectors including Aux3 TseH.

machinery_genes.role: contractile sheath subunit (outer); VipA partner forming the tubular sheath
machinery_genes.quote: VipA/VipB form tubular, cogwheel-like structures that are converted by a threading activity of ClpV into small complexes.
machinery_genes.note: Bonemann et al 2009 EMBO J, V. cholerae. VipB = TssC sheath subunit, essential T6SS member forming the contractile sheath with VipA.

machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase; remodels/recycles the contracted VipA/VipB sheath
machinery_genes.quote: ClpV is crucial for a functional T6SS and interacts through its N-terminal domain with the VipA/VipB complex
machinery_genes.note: Bonemann et al 2009 EMBO J, V. cholerae. ClpV = TssH, the specialized AAA+ ATPase that disassembles the contracted sheath (sheath recycling). Counted per the apparatus brief. Structural basis of ClpV-VipB recognition further detailed in Pietrosiuk et al 2011 J Biol Chem (PMID 21733841, doi 10.1074/jbc.M111.253377).

notes: Aux3 is the fourth (auxiliary) T6SS cluster of pandemic V. cholerae N16961, on chromosome 2 (NC_002506.1), spanning VCA0281-VCA0286. It is a minimal effector-delivery module: integrase (VCA0281), IS5 transposase (VCA0282), PAAR2 (VCA0284), the TseH hydrolase effector (VCA0285 = this instance's curated effector VC_A0285), and its immunity TsiH (VCA0286). The ONLY structural/apparatus gene physically inside Aux3 is PAAR2 (VCA0284, named by Altindis 2015 and Santoriello 2020). Aux3 has no Hcp/VgrG/sheath/baseplate/membrane genes of its own; its effector docks onto the shared T6SS apparatus encoded by the large (main) cluster. Per the separated-paper rule and the T6SS-SPECIAL rule (Hcp/VgrG/PAAR count as machinery), I list the canonical shared V. cholerae N16961 apparatus that delivers the Aux3 effector: the secreted tube (Hcp), the secreted trimeric spike (VgrG-1/-2/-3), the contractile sheath (VipA/VipB = TssB/TssC), and the sheath-recycling ATPase (ClpV = TssH), all named in the founding V. cholerae T6SS papers (Pukatzki 2006/2007; Bonemann 2009). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: TseH effector (VCA0285) and TsiH immunity (VCA0286) as cargo/immunity not machinery; VasH (sigma-54 activator) and VasX (membrane-targeting virulence factor) as regulatory/non-structural; the Aux3 integrase and IS5 transposase as mobile-element recombination machinery, not T6SS apparatus. NOT individually quote-resolved from V. cholerae primary papers and therefore omitted: the baseplate (TssE/F/G/K) and membrane-complex (TssJ/L/M = VasA/VasF/VasK etc.) gene-by-gene names. These exist in the large cluster and are catalogued under the vas nomenclature, but the retrieved primary papers (Pukatzki 2006/2007, Bonemann 2009) name the vas cluster collectively and characterize Hcp/VgrG/VipA/VipB/ClpV individually rather than giving per-gene naming quotes for TssE-M. Status COMPLETE for the secreted tube/spike/tip + sheath + ATPase core that is load-bearing for this benchmark; the baseplate/membrane subcomplex is the documented gap. Lowest-confidence inclusion: none (all listed genes have a direct naming quote tying them to the V. cholerae T6SS). All quotes verbatim from cited PMC full texts / abstracts.
